name: Naturepedia Ocean System Map
description: Machine-readable ocean interaction map connecting solar energy, winds, waves, tides, temperature, salinity, circulation, depth zones, seafloor geology, upwelling, atmosphere exchange, coastal habitats, nutrients, marine food webs, carbon movement, observation, and stewardship.

about.name: Ocean Surface Exchange
about.description: Exchange of solar energy, heat, moisture, gases, and momentum between the atmosphere and ocean surface.

about.name: Ocean Zones
about.description: Vertical and horizontal ocean regions organized by depth, light, temperature, pressure, distance from shore, and seafloor position.

about.name: Seafloor Systems
about.description: Continental shelves, slopes, abyssal plains, ridges, trenches, sediments, and hydrothermal environments beneath the ocean.

about.name: Ocean Circulation
about.description: Connected surface, intermediate, and deep-water movement driven by winds, density differences, planetary rotation, and basin geometry.

about.name: Currents and Gyres
about.description: Organized ocean flow shaped by winds, the Coriolis effect, coastlines, basin boundaries, fronts, and eddies.

about.name: Upwelling and Downwelling
about.description: Vertical water movement that transports nutrients, oxygen, heat, carbon, and dissolved properties between surface and deeper layers.

about.name: Coastal and Estuarine Systems
about.description: Transition systems where rivers, watersheds, tides, sediment, nutrients, salinity gradients, wetlands, and marine waters interact.

about.name: Marine Food Webs
about.description: Biological pathways connecting phytoplankton, microbes, zooplankton, invertebrates, fish, seabirds, marine mammals, predators, and decomposers.

about.name: Ocean Nutrient Cycling
about.description: Movement and recycling of nitrogen, phosphorus, silica, iron, carbon, and other materials through physical and biological ocean processes.

about.name: Ocean Carbon Pathways
about.description: Carbon exchange, biological uptake, respiration, decomposition, particle sinking, deep-water transfer, and sediment burial.

about.name: Ocean Patterns Across Scale
about.description: Connected patterns expressed as ripples, waves, tides, fronts, eddies, gyres, coastlines, basin circulation, and planetary flows.

about.name: Ocean Observation and Stewardship
about.description: Satellite, buoy, profiling-float, underwater-vehicle, wildlife, field, modeling, validation, communication, and stewardship systems.

hasPart.name: Atmosphere–Ocean Exchange Pathway
hasPart.description: Solar heating, winds, evaporation, precipitation, and gas exchange → ocean-surface response.

hasPart.name: Wind-to-Circulation Pathway
hasPart.description: Winds → waves and surface currents → gyres, boundary currents, fronts, and eddies.

hasPart.name: Density Circulation Pathway
hasPart.description: Temperature plus salinity → density differences → sinking, deep-water formation, and ocean-interior circulation.

hasPart.name: Upwelling Productivity Pathway
hasPart.description: Surface-water transport → upwelling → nutrient delivery → phytoplankton productivity and marine food-web response.

hasPart.name: Watershed-to-Ocean Pathway
hasPart.description: Watersheds and rivers → estuaries → sediment, nutrient, freshwater, and organic-material exchange with coastal waters.

hasPart.name: Marine Food-Web Pathway
hasPart.description: Sunlight plus nutrients → phytoplankton → zooplankton → fish, seabirds, marine mammals, and higher predators.

hasPart.name: Biological Carbon Pathway
hasPart.description: Photosynthesis and food-web processing → organic particles → marine snow → deep-water and sediment carbon transfer.

hasPart.name: Hydrothermal Ecosystem Pathway
hasPart.description: Seafloor fractures plus geothermal heat and circulating seawater → mineral-rich discharge → chemosynthetic ecosystems.

hasPart.name: Ocean Pattern Pathway
hasPart.description: Energy, motion, rotation, density, and boundaries → waves, tides, fronts, eddies, gyres, and global circulation patterns.

hasPart.name: Observation-to-Stewardship Pathway
hasPart.description: Distributed observations → field validation → connected models → human interpretation, communication, and stewardship.
